discussion_comments.comment_body:
Good catch — you're right that importing the shared helper alone doesn't activate the guard, and my original commit message overclaimed. The PR description and commit message have been updated to honestly describe this as deduplication-only; the `UnknownErrorTracker` remains dormant infrastructure on both sides (same as `upstream/dev` today, where `executor.ts` declares the interface but never instantiates one either).

**Note for maintainer** (cc'd in the PR description): I deliberately scoped this PR to extraction so the refactor is review-friendly and easy to revert. If you'd prefer this PR also wire up the tracker in `dag-executor.ts` (and possibly `executor.ts`'s callsites) to actually deliver the abort behavior in production, happy to add that on top — just let me know which scope you'd prefer. Otherwise I'd suggest activation as a follow-up PR so the design choices (per-conversation tracker? per-DAG-run? where does it live?) get their own discussion.
Please open a follow-up GitHub issue to track the activation work (wiring UnknownErrorTracker into both executor.ts and dag-executor.ts
Fixed. Added guard: \ — only overrides defaults when detection returns a known forge. Unknown hostnames keep the \ / \ defaults.
Resolved in 8299d48b: binary path overrides are now trimmed and blank COPILOT_BIN_PATH / copilotBinaryPath values fail fast with clear config errors. Added resolver regression coverage.

Resolved in 8299d48b: sendQuery now catches resolver/setup failures before spawning and returns a copilot_cli_exit result chunk instead of throwing. Added regression coverage for a blank COPILOT_BIN_PATH setup failure.

Resolved: removed the discarded first `collect(gen)` from the try block so the generator is only consumed once. Now `await firstNext` is the only expression in the try block; the single `collect(gen)` call in the `if (!primaryError)` branch correctly captures any error that surfaces via a result chunk rather than consuming the generator twice.

Already addressed in commit 85ce222 (QA round 1 fix). The `access()` call is now inside its own `try/catch` — ENOENT returns silently, non-ENOENT (e.g., EACCES) logs a warning and returns without throwing. The `directoryExists` helper is no longer used. Both the access check and the exec are independently non-fatal.

Fixed in 108addfb. Snapshot injected keys before `Object.assign`, restore in the `finally` block. Used `Reflect.deleteProperty` instead of `delete process.env[key]` to satisfy the `@typescript-eslint/no-dynamic-delete` lint rule.

Note: in practice the CLI exits after each invocation so the bleed scenario doesn't occur today, but the cleanup is the right defensive pattern regardless.
